title: Protium multijugum
taxonomy: Protium multijugum is a species within the family Burseraceae. It belongs to the order Sapindales and the class Magnoliopsida. The classification of Protium multijugum is also known to include the kingdom Plantae. This classification places it within the larger group of flowering plants. It is native to Southeast Asia.
uses: Protium multijugum is a plant species that has been traditionally used in various medicinal applications due to its potential health benefits. Protium multijugum contains bioactive compounds that may possess anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which could aid in treating various health conditions. The plant has been used to treat respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne. Additionally, Protium multijugum may have anti-cancer properties, although more research is needed to confirm this. Further studies are necessary to fully understand the medicinal uses and potential therapeutic applications of this plant species.

parts: Protium multijugum. The medicinal parts of Protium multijugum, also known as the Canarium tree, are its resin and bark. The resin of this plant has been traditionally used in various cultures to treat fever, cough, and digestive issues. In some parts of Asia, the bark of Protium multijugum is used to treat various health conditions, including rheumatism and skin problems.
